title: 109 biophysics-recombination partial correlations exceed |r| > 0.08 after GC control — the genome-wide strong-signal census

subject.display: Migration cohort for '109 biophysics-recombination partial correlations exceed |r| > 0.08 after GC con'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Palsson 2025 1 Mb autosomal maps with CO, NCO, and DSB rates (3 outcomes).
All 64 biophysical tracks from Polymer_Evolution phases 1–3.5 at 1 kb, aggregated to 1 Mb means. gc_content reserved as the partial-correlation control.

operations.note: 64 tracks × 3 outcomes = 192 partial-correlation tests. The LabeledValue[] statistic reports the signed top-3 per outcome (9 total) plus the total count > 0.08.

statistics.name: count_partial_r_abs_gt_008
statistics.note: 109 of 192 tests clear |partial r| > 0.08 after GC residualization.

statistics.name: top_signals_signed_per_outcome
statistics.note: Signed top-3 partial Spearman r per outcome (CO and NCO shown; DSB top-3 omitted pending RESULT.md expansion). LabeledValue[] vector — the per-window detail lives in results/a1_partial_correlations.csv.

statistics.name: max_abs_partial_spearman_across_192_tests
statistics.note: Derived: max(|partial r|) over the 192-test grid. Achieved by NCO × cpg_density.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts license the census-level claim: (1) at least 20 tests clearing the |partial r| > 0.08 threshold rules out the chance-only hypothesis under 192 tests (naive Bonferroni-adjusted noise floor would expect <1 false positive above 0.08 at n ≈ 2600 windows); (2) the strongest signal (NCO × cpg_density = +0.37) is well above the 0.08 threshold and the isochore-only null (pre-registered < 0.05). The 20-test lower bound is deliberately well below the observed 109 so the claim is the 'strong-signal census is non-empty' version; the 109 value itself is the descriptive headline.
inference.failure_mode: If the count dropped below ~20, the isochore-only null would become compatible with the data (just noise above 0.08). If max(|partial r|) dropped below 0.08, the entire A1 analysis returns no signals.

conclusion.assertion: Across 64 biophysical tracks × 3 meiotic outcomes at 1 Mb autosomal resolution, 109 partial Spearman correlations exceed |r| > 0.08 after GC residualization — a pervasive GC-independent biophysics–recombination signal. The strongest per-outcome partial correlations are NCO × cpg_density (+0.37), NCO × meth_sensitivity (+0.34), NCO × quantization_map (+0.29), CO × curvature (-0.24), CO × periodicity_power (+0.22), and CO × groove_width (+0.22).

scope.scale_note: Census-level claim at 1 Mb. Per-track sign-flip claims (A2) are separate child fixtures. Sub-isochore resolution (A5, oNCO 1 kb) is a pending bolstering fixture, not covered here.

confidence.note: Family-wise significance reported per-test in results/a1_partial_correlations.csv.

external_assumptions.statement: |partial r| > 0.08 threshold is a conservative noise floor at ~2600 autosomal 1 Mb windows with Spearman ranks; pre-registered in DESIGN.md § A1 as the signal-vs-noise cutoff.
GC residualization (via partial correlation) is the canonical composition control; residual confounding from CpG / stacking is small per the GC-Arrangement decomposition (Exp 21).
